generator_prompt_injection:
REAL TEST-TAKER INSIGHTS (sourced from r/Sat, updated 2026-06-11):
- Coverage: Real test-takers report this exam covers breadth over depth. Stay at college 101 level on each topic.
- High-emphasis topics (per real students): math, english, sat scores.
- Students struggle most with: inferences, command of evidence, words in context. Generate proportionally more on these.
- Wording style: ~9% of items should use realistic exam-style indirection (NOT unfair ambiguity).
- Difficulty target: 15% easy / 50% medium / 35% hard. avg pass score 1315 — harder test.
- Authenticity bar: students compare us to A Song of Ice and Fire series, The Godfather, cough drops. Match that register.

popup_tips.text: On SAT READING WRITING, real test-takers say breadth beats depth. Aim to recognize many topics at a 101 level rather than mastering a few in deep detail.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat

popup_tips.text: Heaviest-weighted topics on the actual SAT READING WRITING exam (per recent test-takers): math, english, sat scores. Spend more time here.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat

popup_tips.text: Where SAT READING WRITING students reported struggling most: inferences, command of evidence, words in context. Make sure you can handle these before exam day.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat

popup_tips.text: Resources real SAT READING WRITING test-takers credited: A Song of Ice and Fire series and The Godfather. Pair with practice questions.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat
